title: Aster oligocephalus
intro: Aster oligocephalus, also known as the few-headed aster, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, wounds, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Aster oligocephalus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Aster oligocephalus is commonly prepared into infusions, decoctions, and tinctures to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Aster oligocephalus is a plant species within the family Asteraceae. It is a flowering plant that belongs to the genus Aster, which includes many species of daisies and asters. The classification of Aster oligocephalus is further divided into the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta. As a member of the Asteraceae family, it is closely related to other plants such as chrysanthemums and sunflowers. The exact classification of Aster oligocephalus may vary depending on the taxonomic system used.
